channel.title: Publicaciones on Sitio web de Manuel Carlevaro
channel.description: Recent content in Publicaciones on Sitio web de Manuel Carlevaro

item.title: Intruder in a two-dimensional granular system: Effects of dynamic and static basal friction on stick-slip and clogging dynamics
item.description: We present simulation results for an intruder pulled through a two-dimensional granular system by a spring using a model designed to mimic the experiments described by Kozlowski et al.

item.title: Microwave tomography with phaseless data on the calcaneus by means of artificial neural networks
item.description: The aim of this study is to use a multilayer perceptron (MLP) artificial neural network (ANN) for phaseless imaging the human heel (modeled as a bilayer dielectric media: bone and surrounding tissue) and the calcaneus cross-section size and location using a two-dimensional (2D) microwave tomographic array.

item.title: Dynamics of a grain-scale intruder in a two-dimensional granular medium with and without basal friction
item.description: We report on a series of experiments in which a grain-sized intruder is pushed by a spring through a two-dimensional granular material composed of photoelastic disks in a Couette geometry.

item.title: Sensitivity analysis on imaging the calcaneus using microwaves
item.description: The bone quality is associated with changes in its dielectric properties (permittivity and conductivity).

item.title: Uncertainties and temperature correction in molecular dynamic simulations of dielectric properties of condensed polar systems
item.description: A robust, simple and fast procedure for the calculation of uncertainties in static relative permittivity (εs) computed via molecular dynamics (MD) is proposed.

item.title: Phaseless Microwave Imaging Of Dielectric Cylinders: An Artificial Neural Networks-Based Approach
item.description: An inverse method for parameters estimation of dielectric cylinders (dielectric properties, location, and radius) from amplitude-only microwave information is presented.

item.title: Numerical simulation of proppant transport in a planar fracture. A study of perforation placement and injection strategy
item.description: We present numerical simulations, based on a CFD–DEM approach, of the transport and settlement of proppant in a planar vertical cell that mimics a hydraulic fracture.

item.title: Clogging in two-dimensions: effect of particle shape
item.description: We study the discharge of a two-dimensional silo through small openings via discrete element simulations.

item.title: Clustering and percolation theory for continuum systems: Clusters with nonspecific bonds and a residence time in their definition
item.description: In this article, dedicated to the memory of Lesser Blum, we develop a theory for the physical clusters which were introduced some time ago in our group (J.

item.title: Effect of the trabecular bone microstructure on measuring its thermal conductivity: A computer modeling-based study
item.description: The objective of this work is to quantify the relation between the value of the effective thermal conductivity of trabecular bone and its microstructure and marrow content.

item.title: Behavior of H-FABP-fatty acid complex in a protein crystal simulation
Effects of Cancellous Bone Dielectric Variability on Microwaves Detection Feasibility. A Simulation Study
item.description: The biological variability and quality of trabecular bone yield variability of its dielectric properties (permittivity and conductivity).

item.title: Uncertainties in permittivities computed from molecular dynamics simulations and temperature correction of dielectric properties of condensed polar systems
Clustering and percolation theory for continuum systems: Clusters with nonspecific bonds and a residence time in their definition
Sensitivity analysis on imaging the calcaneus using microwaves
Chirality in a quaternionic representation of the genetic code
item.description: A quaternionic representation of the genetic code, previously reported by the authors (BioSystems 141 (10–19), 2016), is updated in order to incorporate chirality of nucleotide bases and amino acids.

item.title: Structure of force networks in tapped particulate systems of disks and pentagons. I. Clusters and loops
item.description: The force network of a granular assembly, defined by the contact network and the corresponding contact forces, carries valuable information about the state of the packing.

item.title: Structure of force networks in tapped particulate systems of disks and pentagons. II. Persistence analysis
item.description: In the companion paper [Pugnaloni et al., Phys. Rev. E 93, 062902 (2016)], we use classical measures based on force probability density functions (PDFs), as well as Betti numbers (quantifying the number of components, related to force chains, and loops), to describe the force networks in tapped systems of disks and pentagons.

item.title: Quaternionic representation of the genetic code
item.description: Abstract A heuristic diagram of the evolution of the standard genetic code is presented.

item.title: Flow rate of polygonal grains through a bottleneck: Interplay between shape and size
item.description: We report two-dimensional simulations of circular and polygonal grains passing through an aperture at the bottom of a silo.

item.title: Modeling of the dielectric properties of trabecular bone samples at microwave frequency
item.description: In this paper, the dielectric properties of human trabecular bone are evaluated under physiological condition in the microwave range.

item.title: Effect of particle shape and fragmentation on the response of particle dampers
item.description: A particle damper (PD) is a device that can attenuate mechanical vibrations thanks to the dissipative collisions between grains contained in a cavity attached to the vibrating structure.

item.title: Exact predictions from the Edwards ensemble versus realistic simulations of tapped narrow two-dimensional granular columns
item.description: We simulate, via a discrete element method, the tapping of a narrow column of disks under gravity.

item.title: Is there any information on micro-structure in microwave tomography of bone tissue?
item.description: In this work, two-dimensional simulations of the microwave dielectric properties of models with ellipses and realistic models of trabecular bone tissue are performed.

item.title: Nonlinear dynamic analysis of an optimal particle damper
item.description: We study the dynamical behavior of a single degree of freedom mechanical system with a particle damper.

item.title: Effect of particle shape and fragmentation on the response of particle dampers
item.description: A particle damper (PD) is a device that can attenuate mechanical vibrations thanks to the dissipative collisions between grains contained in a cavity attached to the vibrating structure.

item.title: Plausible binding Mode of the Active $\alpha 4 \beta 1$ antagonist, MK-0617, determined by Docking and Free Energy Calculations
Plausible binding Mode of the Active $\alpha 4 \beta 1$ antagonist, MK-0617, determined by Docking and Free Energy Calculations
item.description: In the last years, the development of small molecule antagonists of VLA-4 for the treatment of diseases, where cell trafficking and activation are important, has increased considerably.

item.title: Arches and contact forces in a granular pile
item.description: Assemblies of granular particles mechanically stable under their own weight contain arches.

item.title: Genetic algorithm for the pair distribution function of the electron gas
item.description: The pair distribution function of the electron gas is calculated using a parameterized generalization of hypernetted chain approximation with the parameters being obtained by optimizing the system energy with a genetic algorithm.

item.title: Steady state of tapped granular polygons
item.description: The steady state packing fraction of a tapped granular bed is studied for different grain shapes via a discrete element method.

item.title: Quantum hypernetted chain approximation for one-dimensional fermionic systems
item.description: In this comprehensible article we develop, following Fantoni and Rosati formalism, a hypernetted chain approximation for one-dimensional systems of fermions.

item.title: Nonmonotonic reversible branch in four model granular beds subjected to vertical vibration
item.description: We present results from four independent models of a granular assembly subjected to tapping.

item.title: Continuum percolation of long lifespan clusters in a simple fluid
item.description: We present results on the percolation loci for chemical clusters and physical clusters of long lifespan.

item.title: A genetic algorithm approach to routine gamma spectra analysis
item.description: In this work we present an alternative method for performing routine gamma spectra analysis based on genetic algorithm techniques.

item.title: Honey agri-food chain in Argentina: model and simulation
item.description: This work will show the dynamics involved in the study of the honey agri-food chain in Argentina.

item.title: Generalized mean spherical approximation for a model of water with dipole, quadrupole, and short-range potential of tetrahedral symmetry
item.description: A simplified version of the so-called Yukagua model of water, proposed and studied via Monte Carlo simulations elsewhere by two of the authors [Physica A 265, 396 (1999)], is considered.

item.title: Modelo Microscópico de Agua Líquida. Aproximación Esférica Media Generalizada
Limitations for competitiveness in Argentinian sunflower agrifood chain
item.description: Argentina is today the first sunflower oil producer and exporter. Its competitiveness relies on being highly efficient in the primary production and industrialization stages.

item.title: Limitations for Efficiency within the Beef Agrifood Chain in Argentina
item.description: In this contribution we present a competitiveness study of the beef agrifood chain in Argentina.

item.title: Solvation properties of non-polar amino acids in water and methanol: a molecular dynamics study
item.description: Solvation properties of the amino acids alanine, valine, leucine and phenylalanine in water and in methanol at infinite dilution have been studied by using molecular dynamics simulations.

item.title: Hydration properties of xylitol: computer simulation
item.description: We present a molecular dynamics simulation of xylitol in SPC/E water using classical Gibbs ensemble molecular dynamics simulation.

item.title: An exponential approximation for continuum percolation in dipolar hard-sphere fluids
item.description: We consider a generalized mean spherical approximation for the pair-connectedness function of a dipolar hard-sphere fluid.

item.title: Percolación Continua en Fluidos Dipolares
